title: symphyotrichum bullatum
intro: Symphyotrichum bullatum, also known as the little white wood's-edge aster, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. It belongs to the Asteraceae family and is often used for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing pain, inflammation, and fever. Symphyotrichum bullatum can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
